task_completion.description: Navigate to the Wazuh web interface
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Wazuh dashboard or main interface is visible in the web browser.

task_completion.description: Access the Agent groups management section
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Agent groups' or 'Groups' management page is open within the Wazuh UI, typically found under Management or Endpoints.

task_completion.description: Initiate the creation of a new group
task_completion.visual_evidence: A modal, dialog, or form specifically for adding or creating a new agent group is visible on the screen.
The exact text 'dmz-servers' is typed into the group name input field.

task_completion.description: Confirm the group was successfully created
task_completion.visual_evidence: A success notification for group creation is displayed, or the group 'dmz-servers' is clearly visible as a new entry in the list of existing agent groups.

integrity.description: The agent used the Wazuh web GUI to create the group
integrity.visual_evidence: Interactions occur within a web browser showing the Wazuh interface, not a terminal window using curl, scripts, or Wazuh API commands.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the actual application
integrity.visual_evidence: The browser address bar shows the correct Wazuh URL, and there is no evidence of a local text editor or mock HTML file being used.

integrity.description: The agent did not use browser developer tools to fake the result
integrity.visual_evidence: The browser developer tools (Inspect Element/Console) are not open, and the UI elements match standard Wazuh styling without manual DOM manipulation.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target agent group name that must be created is exactly 'dmz-servers'.
